content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB, let f(B)=f(B) = f(B)= the number of occurrences of the mode†^\dagger† in BBB.

For example, f([2])=1,f([2,3,3])=2,f([3,3,3,4,4,4])=3f([2]) = 1, f([2, 3, 3]) = 2, f([3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 4]) = 3f([2])=1,f([2,3,3])=2,f([3,3,3,4,4,4])=3.

You’re given an array AAA of length NNN and QQQ queries of the following type:


PPP XXX: Given integers PPP and XXX,

Set AP=XA_P = XAP​=X;
Find the **minimum** value of ∑i=1Nf([A1,A2,…,Ai])\displaystyle{\sum^{N}_{i=1} f([A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_i])}i=1∑N​f([A1​,A2​,…,Ai​]) for any **rearrangement** of updated AAA.


Please note that the queries are **interdependent**, meaning the element updation made during one query will be reflected in subsequent queries.

†^\dagger† Mode of an array is the element with maximum frequency. If multiple elements have maximum frequency, all of them are said to be modes. For example, array [3,3,3,4,4,4][3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 4][3,3,3,4,4,4] has mode 333 and 444, as both have frequency 333.


Input Format

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ - the length of the array and the number of queries, respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the array.
The next QQQ lines, each contain two space-separated integers PPP and XXX, denoting the query.


Output Format
For each query, output on a new line, the answer to the query.
